title: lontarus domestica
side_effects: Lontarus domestica can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, including skin irritation, hives, and respiratory issues. Ingestion of the plant's extracts can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils can lead to headaches, dizziness, and even seizures in severe cases. If not handled correctly, the plant's sap can also cause eye and skin burns, requiring immediate medical attention.
